pr.title: Link and ninja apply HSD Bytecode
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Marked sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c as Matching in configure.py and adjusted GALE01 symbol metadata for HSD ByteCode data constants from broad/global placeholder names to local compiler-style labels. The decomp.dev report showed linked code improved by 5640 bytes, linked data by 232 bytes, matched data by 56 bytes, and one new match for main/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode .sdata2 reaching 100%.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entry for sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Renamed several HSD ByteCode-related .data string symbols at 0x804074F0 through 0x80407574 from lbl_804074F0-style global labels to local @393, @394, @395, @398, @399, @404, and @407 symbols.
Added a local ...data.0 label at .data:0x804074F0 while preserving the existing hsdClass global object and existing ...data.0 label at .data:0x80407590.
Changed HSD_ByteCode_804D5F98 and HSD_ByteCode_804D5F9C in .sdata to local @396 and @397 symbols; the 0x804D5F98 entry became size 0x1 instead of size 0x4, while @397 remained a 0x6 string.

Changed HSD_ByteCode_804DE7A0 through HSD_ByteCode_804DE7D0 in .sdata2 from global HSD_ByteCode_* names to local @392, @400, @401, @402, @403, @405, @406, and @409 constants, keeping float/double size and alignment annotations.

The automated decomp.dev report recorded GALE01 linked code at 42.55% (+0.15%, +5640 bytes), matched data at 41.43% (+56 bytes), linked data at 35.03% (+232 bytes), and one new .sdata2 match for main/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode from 87.50% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: The PR did not just flip bytecode.c to Matching; it also corrected symbol-map names, scopes, sizes, and labels so the linked object could be applied cleanly by the build/ninja pipeline.
Using compiler-style local @NNN labels for file-local string and floating constants avoided exporting artificial HSD_ByteCode_* global names that were only placeholders in symbols.txt.
Preserving alignment and data type metadata for .sdata2 float/double constants while changing their names/scopes minimized the risk of shifting data layout.
The small configure.py change was backed by symbol-map cleanup and an automated match report, making the matching-state transition auditable.
decomp_lessons: Before promoting an object from NonMatching to Matching, verify that any section-local constants in symbols.txt use local compiler-style labels rather than invented globals.
For baselib object matching, data-symbol scope can matter as much as C code: globals such as HSD_ByteCode_804DE7A0 may need to become local @NNN constants for the object to match and link cleanly.
When ninja-applying a newly matched object, inspect .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 together; this PR touched all three for bytecode.c.

Size corrections on small data symbols should be captured in symbols.txt when the compiler emits a smaller object than the placeholder map indicated, as with 0x804D5F98 changing from size 0x4 to size 0x1.
A successful Matching flip should be validated by the decomp.dev unit report, especially the per-unit before/after percentage for the affected object.
naming_conventions: Use @NNN local labels for compiler-generated local constants and strings tied to a translation unit.
Avoid lbl_80xxxxxx global labels for local string literals once the owning object and compiler numbering are known.
Avoid HSD_ByteCode_80xxxxxx global names for local .sdata/.sdata2 constants unless evidence shows they are true globals.
Keep true named globals such as hsdClass, seed, seed_ptr, HSD_QuatLib_804DE760, HSD_TExp_804DE7D8, HSD_TExp_804DE7E0, and HSD_TExpDag_804DE7E8 as globals when the diff does not implicate them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promote Object(NonMatching, "sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c") to Object(Matching, ...) only after symbol-map adjustments let the object link/match.
Use symbols.txt scope changes from global to local to reflect TU-local constants and avoid mismatches from incorrect external symbol treatment.
For .sdata2 constants, preserve align:4 for floats and align:8 for doubles while changing only the symbol identity/scope needed for matching.
Automated report evidence indicates the tactic completed the bytecode .sdata2 match, improving main/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode .sdata2 from 87.50% to 100.00%.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only comment was the decomp.dev bot report confirming metric improvements and one new match for main/sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode .sdata2.
searchable_terms: Link and ninja apply HSD Bytecode
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that flip sysdolphin/baselib objects from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
Search for symbol-map changes that rename HSD_* address placeholders to @NNN local labels.
Check later PRs touching sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c for source-level cleanup after this matching/linking PR.
Look for remaining HSD_ByteCode_* symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt to see whether any true globals or placeholders remain.
